hook: Your track has no ISRC code?

slides.headline: Your track has no ISRC code?
That's the first thing we fix at SyncMaster.
slides.lede: Here's exactly why it matters more than almost anything else in your catalogue. ↓ · ISRC = International Standard Recording Code

slides.headline: A unique identifier assigned to each individual recording.
slides.lede: Think of it as a barcode for your music. · Why it matters for sync specifically:

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: It identifies your recording in global licensing databases · It links your track to royalty collection societies worldwide (SAMRO, COSON, SOCAN, PRS, ASCAP, and more) · Without it, performance royalties from any placement can't reach you · Without it, your track can't be properly catalogued in sync libraries
list_items: It identifies your recording in global licensing databases
It links your track to royalty collection societies worldwide (SAMRO, COSON, SOCAN, PRS, ASCAP, and more)
Without it, performance royalties from any placement can't reach you
Without it, your track can't be properly catalogued in sync libraries

slides.headline: A placement without an ISRC = money you'll never collect.
Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: Through your national music body: · RISA (South Africa) · COSON (Nigeria) · MCSK (Kenya)

slides.headline:
Through your distributor:
→ DistroKid, TuneCore, CD Baby all assign ISRCs

Cost: Free or minimal.
Time: 24–72 hours in most cases.
Every track in the SyncMaster catalogue has an ISRC.
slides.lede: Every composer we onboard gets this sorted in the first call. · Questions? DM us. Or apply: link in bio.

slides.headline: Open briefs. Every Tuesday.
slides.lede: Composers: apply once, get matched for life. Supervisors: shortlist in 48h.
